text.title: Pain Point Discovery Framework
text.summary: How to define research scope, mine pain points from 4 source tiers, and extract root causes using 5-Whys analysis.

rule.statement: Define audience, context, and domain before searching; never begin mining without a written scope statement.
rule.rationale: Vague scope produces unactionable findings. "Founders" is too broad; "solo founders, US, post-launch B2B SaaS, under $10k MRR" produces searchable, comparable pain points.

example.code: Audience: Freelance web developers Context: Client project management Domain: Remote services Goal: Find 20 pain points in 4 hours

section.title: Source Tiers
section.p: Tier 1 (highest signal): direct complaints — Reddit sorted by top/controversial, Twitter/X rants, Product Hunt negative reviews, G2/Capterra 1-3 star reviews, App Store 1-2 star reviews.
Tier 2 (problem indicators): questions — Quora "how to" queries, Stack Overflow duplicates, subreddit FAQ sections, Google "People Also Ask" results.
Tier 3 (communities): Slack groups, Discord servers, Facebook groups, Indie Hackers discussions.
Tier 4 (jobs and gigs): Upwork job posts, Fiverr popular gigs, LinkedIn job listings for "looking for someone to...".

rule.statement: Mine at least 3 source tiers per research session; never rely on a single source type.
rule.rationale: Reddit alone is biased toward complainers. Mixing Tier 1 complaints with Tier 2 questions and Tier 4 gigs triangulates the real pain landscape.

rule.statement: Capture the verbatim quote and URL for every pain point; never substitute an agent's paraphrase.
rule.rationale: Paraphrases lose signal and cannot be re-validated. Every quote must link to an actual URL or it is worthless for downstream validation.

rule.statement: Run 5-Whys analysis for the top 3 scored pain points; the first-order pain is rarely the right thing to build.
rule.rationale: Building for first-order symptoms produces features, not products. Root causes reveal structural opportunities that competitors also miss.

example.code: Pain: "I spend 3 hours/week on expense reports" Why 1: Reports are manual Why 2: System does not auto-import receipts Why 3: No OCR integration Why 4: IT will not approve new tools Why 5: Tool approval process takes 6 months Root cause: Organizational friction in tool adoption

text.reference: Research log template with scope, per-pain entries, and summary table
